title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

title: Congress returns as scrutiny grows over Trump's Iran ceasefire agreement
description: With Congress returning to Washington, President Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz faces new scrutiny.

title: An AI proxy war could reshape Congress — before Congress reshapes AI
description: The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.

title: Voters weigh what kind of Democrat they want for Utah's new, blue congressional seat
description: The reliably Republican state has its first blue-leaning seat and Utah Democrats are excited for the pickup opportunity. First, they just have to make it through the competitive primary.

title: U.S. and Iran agree to 'roadmap' for final deal, mediators say
description: Qatar and Pakistan said the talks made "encouraging progress" despite a rocky start after Trump made threats to "hit Iran very hard again" while the U.S. and Iranian negotiators met in Switzerland.

title: Trump claims vandals damaged D.C. Reflecting Pool, and says it will be drained again
description: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.

title: For cash-strapped farmers, deal to end Iran fighting comes too late
description: Some of the president’s strongest supporters are hurting as midterm elections approach.

title: Week in politics: continued negotiations with Iran; G7 summit; Reflecting Pool issues
description: There continues to be uncertainty over negotiations. At the same time, the Trump administration continues to aggravate allies.

title: With the opening of the Obama Presidential Center, NPR's Don Gonyea looks back on covering the former president
description: The opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago is a full circle moment for at least one the journalists who covered his political rise.

title: Trump's job approval rating has dropped to 36%, a new NPR/ PBS News/Marist poll shows
description: President Trump's numbers are falling, as many Americans harbor concerns about the economy. His approval now stands at 36% — according to the latest NPR/ PBS News/Marist Poll — a new low.

title: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine explains why he called for abolishing the state's death penalty"
description: Ohio's Governor says that his state should end the death penalty. NPR's Scott Simon talks to Gov. Mike DeWine, a Republican, about why he changed his mind on a practice he once supported.
